title: Hopkinton police seeking information after ‘suspicious video’ of person walking into roadway at night circulates online
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Hopkinton police investigate a video showing a person in a construction vest entering a road, causing a vehicle to swerve.
The incident occurred off Route 85 in Hopkinton around 11:30 p.m., involving a person in a Halloween mask and holding a shovel.
Authorities urge anyone with information on the person in the video to contact Hopkinton police at 508-497-3401.

title: Boston City Council approves $1.7 billion schools budget that cuts hundreds of jobs
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council approved a $1.73 billion budget for Boston Public Schools, cutting over 400 student-facing jobs.
Declining enrollment and rising health care costs prompted job cuts, affecting paraprofessionals and support staff.
Boston Teachers Union opposed the budget, advising state aid and reserve funds to prevent job cuts.

title: Boozy ice cream is coming to Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Tipsy Scoop plans to open its first Boston location at 59 Dartmouth St. in the South End next spring.
The company will offer catering and delivery of its alcohol-infused ice cream, available to those 21 and older.
Tipsy Scoop's owner, Kimberly Medina, highlights her Boston roots and plans to transform the former retail space.

title: Kevin McHale says Larry Bird would light up the modern NBA like a ‘five-alarm fire’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale believes Larry Bird would excel in today's NBA, comparing him favorably to Luka Doncic.
Bird's achievements include three NBA titles, three MVPs, and a career average of .509 field goal percentage.
McHale highlights Bird's versatility and durability, asserting sports science would enhance his performance today.

title: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleaded not guilty to voluntary manslaughter in the shooting of Stephenson King Jr. in Roxbury.
Prosecutors allege O’Malley used excessive force, while his defense claims it was necessary due to King's noncompliance.
O’Malley, the first Boston officer charged in a fatal on-duty shooting in decades, was released on personal recognizance pending trial.

title: Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Embrace Boston acquired a 35,000-square-foot property at 33-41 West St. for a new civic center in Downtown Crossing.
The civic center will feature rotating art exhibitions, a media production space, and community gathering areas.
The center aims to open by Boston's 400th anniversary in 2030, funded by donors and various financial support.

title: Man allegedly points gun during road rage incident in Bellingham
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Fahey, 61, from Franklin, allegedly pointed a gun during a road rage incident in Bellingham.
Fahey faces charges of assault with a dangerous weapon, carrying a firearm intoxicated, and disturbing the peace.
He was arrested near the 99 Restaurant parking lot and booked at the Bellingham Police Department.

title: A 21-year-old accessed a lake limited to Hopkinton residents. A 70-year-old allegedly confronted him and is now charged with attempted murder.
author: John Waller
bullets: A 70-year-old Hopkinton man was charged with attempted murder following a dispute over lake access at Sandy Beach.
Police say Steven Dana forcibly held a 21-year-old with crutches under water during a physical altercation.
Dana faces additional charges of strangulation and assault on a disabled person, with arraignment in Framingham District Court.

title: Review & setlist: With Ben Folds, Boston Pops proves it’s capable of anything
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston Pops Orchestra accompanied young competition winners and Ben Folds in a dynamic concert at Symphony Hall on June 3, 2026.
High school musicians from Massachusetts performed alongside the Pops, showcasing compositions including Rachmaninoff and original pieces.
Ben Folds's set included songs like 'Capable of Anything' and 'The Luckiest,' blending his style with the orchestra's grand accompaniment.

title: Waterfront Quincy house with Boston skyline views — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The waterfront Quincy house offers Boston skyline views and features a Colonial style built in 1930.
The property has 2,256 square feet, 4 bedrooms, 2 full bathrooms, and public sewer/water services.
The annual taxes are $9,313, and the listing price options are $1.45M, $1.35M, and $1.25M.
